speaker: KING
text: The Florentines and Senoys are by th’ ears,

speaker: KING
text: Have fought with equal fortune, and continue

speaker: KING
text: A braving war.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: So ’tis reported, sir.

speaker: KING
text: Nay, ’tis most credible. We here receive it

speaker: KING
text: A certainty vouched from our cousin Austria,

speaker: KING
text: With caution that the Florentine will move us

speaker: KING
text: For speedy aid, wherein our dearest friend

speaker: KING
text: Prejudicates the business and would seem

speaker: KING
text: To have us make denial.

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: His love and wisdom,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Approved so to your Majesty, may plead

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: For amplest credence.

speaker: KING
text: He hath armed our answer,

speaker: KING
text: And Florence is denied before he comes.

speaker: KING
text: Yet for our gentlemen that mean to see

speaker: KING
text: The Tuscan service, freely have they leave

speaker: KING
text: To stand on either part.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: It well may serve

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: A nursery to our gentry, who are sick

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: For breathing and exploit.

speaker: KING
text: What’s he comes here?

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: It is the Count Rossillion, my good lord,

speaker:
FIRST
               
              LORD
text: Young Bertram.

speaker: KING
text: Youth, thou bear’st thy father’s face.

speaker: KING
text: Frank nature, rather curious than in haste,

speaker: KING
text: Hath well composed thee. Thy father’s moral parts

speaker: KING
text: Mayst thou inherit too. Welcome to Paris.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: My thanks and duty are your Majesty’s.

speaker: KING
text: I would I had that corporal soundness now

speaker: KING
text: As when thy father and myself in friendship

speaker: KING
text: First tried our soldiership. He did look far

speaker: KING
text: Into the service of the time and was

speaker: KING
text: Discipled of the bravest. He lasted long,

speaker: KING
text: But on us both did haggish age steal on

speaker: KING
text: And wore us out of act. It much repairs me

speaker: KING
text: To talk of your good father. In his youth

speaker: KING
text: He had the wit which I can well observe

speaker: KING
text: Today in our young lords; but they may jest

speaker: KING
text: Till their own scorn return to them unnoted

speaker: KING
text: Ere they can hide their levity in honor.

speaker: KING
text: So like a courtier, contempt nor bitterness

speaker: KING
text: Were in his pride or sharpness; if they were,

speaker: KING
text: His equal had awaked them, and his honor,

speaker: KING
text: Clock to itself, knew the true minute when

speaker: KING
text: Exception bid him speak, and at this time

speaker: KING
text: His tongue obeyed his hand. Who were below him

speaker: KING
text: He used as creatures of another place

speaker: KING
text: And bowed his eminent top to their low ranks,

speaker: KING
text: Making them proud of his humility,

speaker: KING
text: In their poor praise he humbled. Such a man

speaker: KING
text: Might be a copy to these younger times,

speaker: KING
text: Which, followed well, would demonstrate them now

speaker: KING
text: But goers backward.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: His good remembrance, sir,

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Lies richer in your thoughts than on his tomb.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: So in approof lives not his epitaph

speaker: BERTRAM
text: As in your royal speech.

speaker: KING
text: Would I were with him! He would always say—

speaker: KING
text: Methinks I hear him now; his plausive words

speaker: KING
text: He scattered not in ears, but grafted them

speaker: KING
text: To grow there and to bear. “Let me not live”—

speaker: KING
text: This his good melancholy oft began

speaker: KING
text: On the catastrophe and heel of pastime,

speaker: KING
text: When it was out—“Let me not live,” quoth he,

speaker: KING
text: “After my flame lacks oil, to be the snuff

speaker: KING
text: Of younger spirits, whose apprehensive senses

speaker: KING
text: All but new things disdain, whose judgments are

speaker: KING
text: Mere fathers of their garments, whose constancies

speaker: KING
text: Expire before their fashions.” This he wished.

speaker: KING
text: I, after him, do after him wish too,

speaker: KING
text: Since I nor wax nor honey can bring home,

speaker: KING
text: I quickly were dissolvèd from my hive

speaker: KING
text: To give some laborers room.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: You’re lovèd, sir.

speaker:
SECOND
               
              LORD
text: They that least lend it you shall lack you first.

speaker: KING
text: I fill a place, I know ’t.—How long is ’t, count,

speaker: KING
text: Since the physician at your father’s died?

speaker: KING
text: He was much famed.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Some six months since, my lord.

speaker: KING
text: If he were living, I would try him yet.—

speaker: KING
text: Lend me an arm.—The rest have worn me out

speaker: KING
text: With several applications. Nature and sickness

speaker: KING
text: Debate it at their leisure. Welcome, count.

speaker: KING
text: My son’s no dearer.

speaker: BERTRAM
text: Thank your Majesty.
